message.content: Explain @offmute/ and trace a path from initial input through its main code flow

1→{
     2→  "name": "offmute",
     3→  "version": "0.1.11",
     4→  "author": "Hrishi Olickel <twitter-@hrishioa> (https://olickel.com)",
     5→  "repository": {
     6→    "type": "git",
     7→    "url": "git+https://github.com/southbridgeai/offmute.git"
     8→  },
     9→  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
    10→  "module": "./dist/index.js",
    11→  "bin": {
    12→    "offmute": "./dist/run.js"
    13→  },
    14→  "devDependencies": {
    15→    "@swc/core": "^1.7.40",
    16→    "@types/bun": "^1.1.12",
    17→    "@types/node": "^22.8.4",
    18→    "tsup": "^8.3.5",
    19→    "typescript": "^5.6.3"
    20→  },
    21→  "exports": {
    22→    ".": {
    23→      "import": {
    24→        "types": "./dist/index.d.cts",
    25→        "default": "./dist/index.js"
    26→      },
    27→      "require": {
    28→        "types": "./dist/index.d.cts",
    29→        "default": "./dist/index.cjs"
    30→      }
    31→    }
    32→  },
    33→  "description": "An experiment in meeting transcription and diarization with just an LLM.",
    34→  "files": [
    35→    "dist",
    36→    "package.json"
    37→  ],
    38→  "license": "Apache-2.0",
    39→  "scripts": {
    40→    "build": "tsup src/index.ts src/run.ts && tsc --emitDeclarationOnly --declaration --declarationDir dist && mv dist/index.d.ts dist/index.d.mts && cp dist/index.d.mts dist/index.d.cts"
    41→  },
    42→  "type": "module",
    43→  "types": "./dist/index.d.cts",
    44→  "dependencies": {
    45→    "@google/generative-ai": "^0.21.0",
    46→    "chalk": "^5.3.0",
    47→    "cli-progress": "^3.12.0",
    48→    "commander": "^12.1.0",
    49→    "fluent-ffmpeg": "^2.1.3"
    50→  }
    51→}

1→# npx offmute 🎙️ 2→ 3→
4→ 5→[![NPM version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/offmute.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/offmute) 6→[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 7→ 8→**Intelligent meeting transcription and analysis using Google's Gemini models** 9→ 10→[Features](#-features) • [Quick Start](#-quick-start) • [Installation](#-installation) • [Usage](#-usage) • [Advanced](#-advanced-usage) • [How It Works](#-how-it-works) 11→ 12→
13→ 14→## 🚀 Features 15→ 16→- 🎯 **Transcription & Diarization**: Convert audio/video content to text while identifying different speakers 17→- 🎭 **Smart Speaker Identification**: Attempts to identify speakers by name and role when possible 18→- 📊 **Meeting Reports**: Generates structured reports with key points, action items, and participant profiles 19→- 🎬 **Video Analysis**: Extracts and analyzes visual information from video meetings, understand when demos are being displayed 20→- ⚡ **Multiple Processing Tiers**: From budget-friendly to premium processing options 21→- 🔄 **Robust Processing**: Handles long meetings with automatic chunking and proper cleanup 22→- 📁 **Flexible Output**: Markdown-formatted transcripts and reports with optional intermediate outputs 23→- 🔍 **Real-time Progress**: View transcription and report generation progress in real-time 24→- 🎯 **Custom Instructions**: Add your own context or instructions to guide the AI processing 25→- 🧹 **Clean Filesystem**: Temporary files are managed cleanly without cluttering your directories 26→ 27→## 🏃 Quick Start 28→ 29→```bash 30→# Set your Gemini API key 31→export GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here 32→ 33→# Run on a meeting recording (uses Gemini 2.5 Pro by default) 34→npx offmute path/to/your/meeting.mp4 35→ 36→# Use Flash model for faster processing 37→npx offmute path/to/your/meeting.mp4 --model flash 38→``` 39→ 40→## 📦 Installation 41→ 42→### As a CLI Tool 43→ 44→```bash 45→npx offmute 46→``` 47→ 48→### As a Package 49→ 50→```bash 51→npm install offmute 52→``` 53→ 54→## Get Help 55→ 56→``` 57→npx offmute --help 58→``` 59→ 60→`bunx` or `bun` works faster if you have it! 61→ 62→## 💻 Usage 63→ 64→### Command Line Interface 65→ 66→```bash 67→npx offmute [options] 68→``` 69→ 70→Options: 71→ 72→- `-m, --model `: Model selection (pro, flash, flash-lite) [default: "pro"] 73→- `-t, --tier `: [DEPRECATED] Processing tier (first, business, economy, budget, experimental) - use --model instead 74→- `-s, --save-intermediates`: Save intermediate processing files 75→- `-id, --intermediates-dir `: Custom directory for intermediate output 76→- `-sc, --screenshot-count `: Number of screenshots to extract for video [default: 4] 77→- `-ac, --audio-chunk-minutes `: Length of audio chunks in minutes [default: 10] 78→- `-r, --report`: Generate a structured meeting report 79→- `-rd, --reports-dir `: Custom directory for report output 80→- `-i, --instructions `: Custom context or instructions to include in AI prompts 81→ 82→### Model Selection 83→ 84→#### New Simple Model Options (Recommended) 85→- **Pro** (`pro`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Pro for all operations - highest quality 86→- **Flash** (`flash`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash for all operations - balanced performance 87→- **Flash Lite** (`flash-lite`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite for all operations - fastest and most economical 88→ 89→#### Legacy Processing Tiers (Deprecated but still supported) 90→- **First Tier** (`first`): Uses Gemini 2.0 Pro models for all operations 91→- **Business Tier** (`business`): Gemini 2.0 Pro for description and report, Gemini 2.0 Flash for transcription 92→- **Economy Tier** (`economy`): Gemini 2.0 Flash models for all operations 93→- **Budget Tier** (`budget`): Gemini 2.0 Flash for description, Gemini 2.0 Flash Lite for transcription and report 94→- **Experimental Tier** (`experimental`): Uses the cutting-edge Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview model for all operations 95→- **Experimental Budget Tier** (`experimentalBudget`): Uses the cutting-edge Gemini 2.5 Flash Preview model for all operations 96→ 97→### As a Module 98→ 99→```typescript 100→import { 101→ generateDescription, 102→ generateTranscription, 103→ generateReport, 104→} from "offmute"; 105→ 106→// Generate description and transcription 107→const description = await generateDescription(inputFile, { 108→ screenshotModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", 109→ audioModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", 110→ mergeModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", 111→ showProgress: true, 112→ userInstructions: "Focus on technical content and action items", 113→}); 114→ 115→const transcription = await generateTranscription(inputFile, description, { 116→ transcriptionModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", 117→ showProgress: true, 118→ userInstructions: "Add emotions and tone information for each speaker", 119→}); 120→ 121→// Generate a structured report 122→const report = await generateReport( 123→ description.finalDescription, 124→ transcription.chunkTranscriptions.join("\n\n"), 125→ { 126→ model: "gemini-2.5-pro", 127→ reportName: "meeting_summary", 128→ showProgress: true, 129→ userInstructions: "Highlight all action items with bullet points", 130→ } 131→); 132→``` 133→ 134→## 🔧 Advanced Usage 135→ 136→### Intermediate Files and Directories 137→ 138→By default, offmute uses a system temporary directory to store intermediate files and cleans them up when processing completes. If you want to save these files: 139→ 140→```bash 141→# Save intermediates in a hidden .offmute_[filename] directory 142→npx offmute meeting.mp4 --save-intermediates 143→ 144→# Save intermediates in a custom directory 145→npx offmute meeting.mp4 --save-intermediates --intermediates-dir ./processing_files 146→``` 147→ 148→When saved, intermediate files are organized in a clean structure: 149→ 150→``` 151→.offmute_meeting/ 152→├── screenshots/ # Video screenshots 153→├── audio/ # Processed audio chunks 154→├── transcription/ # Per-chunk transcriptions 155→└── report/ # Report generation data 156→``` 157→ 158→### Custom Instructions 159→ 160→You can provide custom instructions to the AI models to focus on specific aspects: 161→ 162→```bash 163→# Use Flash model for faster processing 164→npx offmute meeting.mp4 --model flash 165→ 166→# Use Flash Lite for the most economical option 167→npx offmute long_conference.mp4 --model flash-lite 168→ 169→# Focus on technical details with Pro model (default) 170→npx offmute technical_meeting.mp4 -i "Focus on technical terminology and highlight all action items" 171→ 172→# Improve speaker emotion detection with Flash model 173→npx offmute interview.mp4 --model flash -i "Pay special attention to emotional tone and hesitations" 174→``` 175→ 176→### Real-time Progress Tracking 177→ 178→Offmute now creates output files early in the process and updates them incrementally, allowing you to: 179→ 180→1. See transcription progress in real-time 181→2. Monitor report generation section by section 182→3. Check partial results even for long-running processes 183→ 184→### Model Selection Examples 185→ 186→```bash 187→# Use Pro model for highest quality (default) 188→npx offmute important_meeting.mp4 189→ 190→# Use Flash model for balanced performance 191→npx offmute team_standup.mp4 --model flash 192→ 193→# Use Flash Lite for quick and economical processing 194→npx offmute daily_brief.mp4 --model flash-lite 195→ 196→# Combine with custom instructions for best results 197→npx offmute strategic_call.mp4 --model pro -i "Focus on financial projections and strategic initiatives" 198→``` 199→ 200→The Gemini 2.5 models support expanded token output capabilities, allowing for more detailed and comprehensive results, especially for longer meetings or when generating complex reports. 201→ 202→### Custom Chunk Sizes 203→ 204→Adjust processing for different content types: 205→ 206→```bash 207→# Longer chunks for presentations 208→offmute presentation.mp4 -ac 20 209→ 210→# More screenshots for visual-heavy content 211→offmute workshop.mp4 -sc 8 212→``` 213→ 214→## ⚙️ How It Works 215→ 216→offmute uses a multi-stage pipeline: 217→ 218→1. **Content Analysis** 219→ 220→ - Extracts screenshots from videos at key moments 221→ - Chunks audio into processable segments 222→ - Generates initial descriptions of visual and audio content 223→ 224→2. **Transcription & Diarization** 225→ 226→ - Processes audio chunks with context awareness 227→ - Identifies and labels speakers 228→ - Maintains conversation flow across chunks 229→ - Shows real-time progress in the output file 230→ 231→3. **Report Generation (Spreadfill)** 232→ - Uses a unique "Spreadfill" technique: 233→ 1. Generates report structure with section headings 234→ 2. Fills each section independently using full context 235→ 3. Ensures coherent narrative while maintaining detailed coverage 236→ - Updates report file in real-time as sections are completed 237→ 238→### Metadata Management 239→ 240→Offmute now includes accurate file metadata in outputs: 241→ 242→- File creation and modification dates 243→- Processing timestamp 244→- File size and path information 245→- Custom instructions (when provided) 246→ 247→This provides reliable context without AI guessing incorrect meeting dates/times. 248→ 249→### Spreadfill Technique 250→ 251→The Spreadfill approach helps maintain consistency while allowing detailed analysis: 252→ 253→```typescript 254→// 1. Generate structure 255→const structure = await generateHeadings(description, transcript); 256→ 257→// 2. Fill sections independently 258→const sections = await Promise.all( 259→ structure.sections.map((section) => generateSection(section, fullContext)) 260→); 261→ 262→// 3. Combine into coherent report 263→const report = combineResults(sections); 264→``` 265→ 266→## 🛠️ Requirements 267→ 268→- Node.js 14 or later 269→- ffmpeg installed on your system 270→- Google Gemini API key 271→ 272→## Contributing 273→ 274→You can start in `TODOs.md` to help with things I'm thinking about, or you can steel yourself and check out `PROBLEMS.md`. 275→ 276→Created by [Hrishi Olickel](https://twitter.com/hrishioa) • Support offmute by starring our [GitHub repository](https://github.com/southbridgeai/offmute) 277→

file.content:
# npx offmute 🎙️
[![NPM version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/offmute.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/offmute) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE) **Intelligent meeting transcription and analysis using Google's Gemini models** [Features](#-features) • [Quick Start](#-quick-start) • [Installation](#-installation) • [Usage](#-usage) • [Advanced](#-advanced-usage) • [How It Works](#-how-it-works)
## 🚀 Features - 🎯 **Transcription & Diarization**: Convert audio/video content to text while identifying different speakers - 🎭 **Smart Speaker Identification**: Attempts to identify speakers by name and role when possible - 📊 **Meeting Reports**: Generates structured reports with key points, action items, and participant profiles - 🎬 **Video Analysis**: Extracts and analyzes visual information from video meetings, understand when demos are being displayed - ⚡ **Multiple Processing Tiers**: From budget-friendly to premium processing options - 🔄 **Robust Processing**: Handles long meetings with automatic chunking and proper cleanup - 📁 **Flexible Output**: Markdown-formatted transcripts and reports with optional intermediate outputs - 🔍 **Real-time Progress**: View transcription and report generation progress in real-time - 🎯 **Custom Instructions**: Add your own context or instructions to guide the AI processing - 🧹 **Clean Filesystem**: Temporary files are managed cleanly without cluttering your directories ## 🏃 Quick Start ```bash # Set your Gemini API key export GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here # Run on a meeting recording (uses Gemini 2.5 Pro by default) npx offmute path/to/your/meeting.mp4 # Use Flash model for faster processing npx offmute path/to/your/meeting.mp4 --model flash ``` ## 📦 Installation ### As a CLI Tool ```bash npx offmute ``` ### As a Package ```bash npm install offmute ``` ## Get Help ``` npx offmute --help ``` `bunx` or `bun` works faster if you have it! ## 💻 Usage ### Command Line Interface ```bash npx offmute [options] ``` Options: - `-m, --model `: Model selection (pro, flash, flash-lite) [default: "pro"] - `-t, --tier `: [DEPRECATED] Processing tier (first, business, economy, budget, experimental) - use --model instead - `-s, --save-intermediates`: Save intermediate processing files - `-id, --intermediates-dir `: Custom directory for intermediate output - `-sc, --screenshot-count `: Number of screenshots to extract for video [default: 4] - `-ac, --audio-chunk-minutes `: Length of audio chunks in minutes [default: 10] - `-r, --report`: Generate a structured meeting report - `-rd, --reports-dir `: Custom directory for report output - `-i, --instructions `: Custom context or instructions to include in AI prompts ### Model Selection #### New Simple Model Options (Recommended) - **Pro** (`pro`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Pro for all operations - highest quality - **Flash** (`flash`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash for all operations - balanced performance - **Flash Lite** (`flash-lite`): Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite for all operations - fastest and most economical #### Legacy Processing Tiers (Deprecated but still supported) - **First Tier** (`first`): Uses Gemini 2.0 Pro models for all operations - **Business Tier** (`business`): Gemini 2.0 Pro for description and report, Gemini 2.0 Flash for transcription - **Economy Tier** (`economy`): Gemini 2.0 Flash models for all operations - **Budget Tier** (`budget`): Gemini 2.0 Flash for description, Gemini 2.0 Flash Lite for transcription and report - **Experimental Tier** (`experimental`): Uses the cutting-edge Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview model for all operations - **Experimental Budget Tier** (`experimentalBudget`): Uses the cutting-edge Gemini 2.5 Flash Preview model for all operations ### As a Module ```typescript import { generateDescription, generateTranscription, generateReport, } from "offmute"; // Generate description and transcription const description = await generateDescription(inputFile, { screenshotModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", audioModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", mergeModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", showProgress: true, userInstructions: "Focus on technical content and action items", }); const transcription = await generateTranscription(inputFile, description, { transcriptionModel: "gemini-2.5-pro", showProgress: true, userInstructions: "Add emotions and tone information for each speaker", }); // Generate a structured report const report = await generateReport( description.finalDescription, transcription.chunkTranscriptions.join("\n\n"), { model: "gemini-2.5-pro", reportName: "meeting_summary", showProgress: true, userInstructions: "Highlight all action items with bullet points", } ); ``` ## 🔧 Advanced Usage ### Intermediate Files and Directories By default, offmute uses a system temporary directory to store intermediate files and cleans them up when processing completes. If you want to save these files: ```bash # Save intermediates in a hidden .offmute_[filename] directory npx offmute meeting.mp4 --save-intermediates # Save intermediates in a custom directory npx offmute meeting.mp4 --save-intermediates --intermediates-dir ./processing_files ``` When saved, intermediate files are organized in a clean structure: ``` .offmute_meeting/ ├── screenshots/ # Video screenshots ├── audio/ # Processed audio chunks ├── transcription/ # Per-chunk transcriptions └── report/ # Report generation data ``` ### Custom Instructions You can provide custom instructions to the AI models to focus on specific aspects: ```bash # Use Flash model for faster processing npx offmute meeting.mp4 --model flash # Use Flash Lite for the most economical option npx offmute long_conference.mp4 --model flash-lite # Focus on technical details with Pro model (default) npx offmute technical_meeting.mp4 -i "Focus on technical terminology and highlight all action items" # Improve speaker emotion detection with Flash model npx offmute interview.mp4 --model flash -i "Pay special attention to emotional tone and hesitations" ``` ### Real-time Progress Tracking Offmute now creates output files early in the process and updates them incrementally, allowing you to: 1. See transcription progress in real-time 2. Monitor report generation section by section 3. Check partial results even for long-running processes ### Model Selection Examples ```bash # Use Pro model for highest quality (default) npx offmute important_meeting.mp4 # Use Flash model for balanced performance npx offmute team_standup.mp4 --model flash # Use Flash Lite for quick and economical processing npx offmute daily_brief.mp4 --model flash-lite # Combine with custom instructions for best results npx offmute strategic_call.mp4 --model pro -i "Focus on financial projections and strategic initiatives" ``` The Gemini 2.5 models support expanded token output capabilities, allowing for more detailed and comprehensive results, especially for longer meetings or when generating complex reports. ### Custom Chunk Sizes Adjust processing for different content types: ```bash # Longer chunks for presentations offmute presentation.mp4 -ac 20 # More screenshots for visual-heavy content offmute workshop.mp4 -sc 8 ``` ## ⚙️ How It Works offmute uses a multi-stage pipeline: 1. **Content Analysis** - Extracts screenshots from videos at key moments - Chunks audio into processable segments - Generates initial descriptions of visual and audio content 2. **Transcription & Diarization** - Processes audio chunks with context awareness - Identifies and labels speakers - Maintains conversation flow across chunks - Shows real-time progress in the output file 3. **Report Generation (Spreadfill)** - Uses a unique "Spreadfill" technique: 1. Generates report structure with section headings 2. Fills each section independently using full context 3. Ensures coherent narrative while maintaining detailed coverage - Updates report file in real-time as sections are completed ### Metadata Management Offmute now includes accurate file metadata in outputs: - File creation and modification dates - Processing timestamp - File size and path information - Custom instructions (when provided) This provides reliable context without AI guessing incorrect meeting dates/times. ### Spreadfill Technique The Spreadfill approach helps maintain consistency while allowing detailed analysis: ```typescript // 1. Generate structure const structure = await generateHeadings(description, transcript); // 2. Fill sections independently const sections = await Promise.all( structure.sections.map((section) => generateSection(section, fullContext)) ); // 3. Combine into coherent report const report = combineResults(sections); ``` ## 🛠️ Requirements - Node.js 14 or later - ffmpeg installed on your system - Google Gemini API key ## Contributing You can start in `TODOs.md` to help with things I'm thinking about, or you can steel yourself and check out `PROBLEMS.md`. Created by [Hrishi Olickel](https://twitter.com/hrishioa) • Support offmute by starring our [GitHub repository](https://github.com/southbridgeai/offmute)

content.content:
1→// prettier-ignore
     2→export const AUDIO_DESC_PROMPT = (fileName: string, userInstructions?: string) =>
     3→`Describe this audio in two paragraphs with the following information:
     4→
     5→1. What are the key topics of discussion?
     6→2. Who are the people talking to the best of your knowledge? Describe them, names if possible at all, and their personalities, job functions, etc. Identify them with speaker 1 and 2 if you don't have names.
     7→3. Summarise this conversation in three sentences, like 'This is a discussion between X (who Y) and X (who Y) about ....'
     8→
     9→IMPORTANT: Do NOT guess or include the meeting time, date, or any other metadata that is not explicitly mentioned in the audio. If these details are not clearly stated, do not include them.
    10→
    11→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}File: ${fileName}`
    12→
    13→// prettier-ignore
    14→export const IMAGE_DESC_PROMPT = (fileNames: string, userInstructions?: string) =>
    15→`Provided are some screenshots from a meeting. Can you provide the following information from the image (when available)?
    16→
    17→1. Who the speakers are (if you can see them), their names, descriptions.
    18→2. General emotions of the people involved.
    19→3. Descriptions of anything else shown on the screen or being shared.
    20→4. Any additional information you can infer about the meeting from the information provided.
    21→
    22→IMPORTANT: Do NOT guess or include the meeting time, date, or any other metadata that is not explicitly shown in the images. If these details are not clearly visible, do not include them.
    23→
    24→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}The files are: ${fileNames}`
    25→
    26→// prettier-ignore
    27→export const MERGE_DESC_PROMPT = (descriptions: string[], userInstructions?: string) =>
    28→`
    29→Descriptions:
    30→\`\`\`
    31→${descriptions.join("\n\n")}
    32→\`\`\`
    33→
    34→Here are the descriptions for a meeting, generated from information (audio/images, etc) about it. Remove conflicting information, use your best judgement to infer what happened, and generate a clean, detailed description for the meeting covering the following:
    35→1. Who the participants of the meeting were. Names if possible.
    36→2. Describe the participants. Appearance if possible, function, emotional state, job, etc.
    37→3. What was discussed? What was shown/covered?
    38→4. If this is not a meeting, provide any additional information that can help describe what this is.
    39→
    40→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}IMPORTANT: Do NOT guess or include the meeting time, date, or location unless these details are explicitly mentioned in the audio or shown in the images. Only include factual information that you can directly observe from the provided content.`
    41→
    42→// prettier-ignore
    43→export const TRANSCRIPTION_PROMPT = (description: string, count: number, total: number, previousTranscription: string, userInstructions?: string) =>
    44→`Provided is (${count}/${total}) of the audio of a meeting.
    45→
    46→Here is the general description of the meeting:
    47→${description}
    48→
    49→${previousTranscription ?
    50→`Here is the transcription from the previous chunk:\n\n...${previousTranscription}\n...` : ""}
    51→
    52→Please diarize and transcribe this audio segment with all the speakers identified and named when possible. Add information on emotions, tone, pauses, etc like (silently contemplating, hesitant, etc etc). Format the output as:
    53→~[Speaker Name]~: Transcribed text
    54→
    55→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}${previousTranscription ?
    56→`Continue where the previous transcription left off.` : ""}`
    57→
    58→// prettier-ignore
    59→export const REPORT_HEADINGS_PROMPT = (descriptions: string, transcript: string, userInstructions?: string) =>
    60→`Meeting Descriptions:
    61→\`\`\`
    62→${descriptions}
    63→\`\`\`
    64→
    65→Transcript:
    66→\`\`\`
    67→${transcript}
    68→\`\`\`
    69→
    70→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}Here are the descriptions and transcript of a meeting or call. We want to turn this into a proper one-pager summary document. Respond with json in this typespec representing the headings and subheadings of the final one-page report, with a one-sentence description. Some things to make sure we cover (in their own section or not) are:
    71→* Useful contacts - companies, people etc
    72→* Action items
    73→* Overall flow of the meeting - what was discussed for how long
    74→* Any arguments or pitches made or things presented
    75→* Participant profiles
    76→
    77→Respond with json in this typespec:
    78→\`\`\`typescript
    79→type MeetingSummary = {
    80→  sections: {
    81→    title: string;
    82→    description: string;
    83→    subsections: {
    84→      title: string;
    85→      description: string;
    86→    }[]
    87→  }[];
    88→}
    89→\`\`\``
    90→
    91→export type ReportHeadings = {
    92→  sections: ReportSection[];
    93→};
    94→
    95→export type ReportSection = {
    96→  title: string;
    97→  description: string;
    98→  subsections: {
    99→    title: string;
   100→    description: string;
   101→  }[];
   102→};
   103→
   104→export const REPORT_HEADINGS_JSONSCHEMA = {
   105→  type: "object",
   106→  description: "Meeting report headings and subheadings",
   107→  properties: {
   108→    sections: {
   109→      type: "array",
   110→      items: {
   111→        type: "object",
   112→        properties: {
   113→          title: {
   114→            type: "string",
   115→          },
   116→          description: {
   117→            type: "string",
   118→          },
   119→          subsections: {
   120→            type: "array",
   121→            items: {
   122→              type: "object",
   123→              properties: {
   124→                title: {
   125→                  type: "string",
   126→                },
   127→                description: {
   128→                  type: "string",
   129→                },
   130→              },
   131→              required: ["title", "description"],
   132→            },
   133→          },
   134→        },
   135→        required: ["title", "description", "subsections"],
   136→      },
   137→    },
   138→  },
   139→  required: ["sections"],
   140→};
   141→
   142→// prettier-ignore
   143→export const REPORT_SECTION_GENERATION_PROMPT = (headings: ReportHeadings, section: ReportSection, transcript: string, descriptions: string, userInstructions?: string) =>
   144→`Meeting Descriptions:
   145→\`\`\`
   146→${descriptions}
   147→\`\`\`
   148→
   149→Transcript:
   150→\`\`\`
   151→${transcript}
   152→\`\`\`
   153→
   154→${userInstructions ? `USER INSTRUCTIONS: ${userInstructions}\n\n` : ''}Here are the descriptions and transcript of a meeting or call. We want to turn this into a proper one-pager summary document. Respond with json representing the headings and subheadings of the final one-page report.
   155→
   156→Here are the main sections we want in the report:
   157→${headings.sections.map((sectionName, index) => `${index + 1}. ${sectionName.title} : ${sectionName.description}`).join("\n")}
   158→
   159→We want to write this specific section in markdown:
   160→${section.title}: ${section.description}
   161→
   162→Respond with just the content for this section, without title or description or foreword or introduction. Presume the other sections have been written.
   163→
   164→Respond in Markdown.
   165→`
   166→

content.text: Now let me read the utility files to complete the picture.

## offmute — Explained

**offmute** is a CLI tool and Node library that transcribes and analyzes meeting recordings (audio/video) using Google's Gemini LLMs. It does speaker diarization, generates transcripts, and optionally produces structured meeting reports — all without a dedicated speech-to-text engine.

---

### Architecture Overview

```
src/
├── run.ts            # CLI entrypoint (commander)
├── index.ts          # Library entrypoint (re-exports 3 functions)
├── describe.ts       # Stage 1: Content description
├── transcribe.ts     # Stage 2: Diarized transcription
├── report.ts         # Stage 3: "Spreadfill" report generation
├── prompts.ts        # All LLM prompt templates
└── utils/
    ├── gemini.ts      # Gemini API wrapper (upload, generate, retry, cleanup)
    ├── audio-chunk.ts # ffmpeg: split audio into overlapping MP3 chunks
    ├── screenshot.ts  # ffmpeg: extract evenly-spaced video screenshots
    ├── check-type.ts  # File extension detection (audio vs video)
    ├── ffmpeg-check.ts# Verify ffmpeg is installed
    └── sanitize.ts    # Filesystem-safe name sanitization
```

---

### End-to-End Flow

Starting from `npx offmute meeting.mp4 --model pro --report`:

#### 1. CLI Bootstrap (`run.ts`)
- Checks ffmpeg is available and `GEMINI_API_KEY` is set
- Parses args with `commander` — resolves model selection (e.g. `pro` → `gemini-2.5-pro` for all three stages)
- If input is a directory, recursively finds all audio/video files
- Calls `processFile()` for each file sequentially

#### 2. Stage 1 — Description (`describe.ts:generateDescription`)
Two things happen **in parallel** via `Promise.all`:

- **Screenshots** (video only): `extractVideoScreenshots` uses ffmpeg to grab N evenly-spaced frames → uploads them to Gemini via `GoogleAIFileManager` → sends `IMAGE_DESC_PROMPT` asking "who are the speakers, what's on screen?" → returns `imageDescription`
- **Audio analysis**: `processAudioFile` uses ffmpeg to split the audio into overlapping MP3 chunks (default 10min, 1min overlap) and a separate "tag sample" (first 20min) → uploads the tag sample to Gemini → sends `AUDIO_DESC_PROMPT` asking "who's talking, what are the topics?" → returns `audioDescription`

Then a **merge step** sends both descriptions to Gemini with `MERGE_DESC_PROMPT` to produce a single coherent `finalDescription` of the meeting.

#### 3. Stage 2 — Transcription (`transcribe.ts:generateTranscription`)
Processes audio chunks **sequentially** (to maintain conversational context):

- For each chunk, builds `TRANSCRIPTION_PROMPT` with: the merged description, chunk number, and the last 20 lines of the previous chunk's transcription
- Uploads the chunk MP3 to Gemini, generates diarized text in `~[Speaker Name]~: text` format
- Incrementally writes to `<filename>_transcription.md` so users can watch progress in real time
- The output file includes metadata (file dates, size), the description, audio/visual analysis, and full transcription

#### 4. Stage 3 — Report (`report.ts:generateReport`, optional `--report`)
Uses the **Spreadfill** technique:

1. **Generate structure**: Sends description + transcript to Gemini with `REPORT_HEADINGS_PROMPT` and a JSON schema → gets back section titles/descriptions (action items, participant profiles, contacts, etc.)
2. **Fill sections independently**: For each section, sends the full transcript + description + section spec via `REPORT_SECTION_GENERATION_PROMPT` → generates that section's markdown content
3. **Incremental file updates**: The report `.md` file is created early with placeholders, then each section is swapped in as it completes
4. **Finalize**: Strips progress indicators from the file

#### 5. Cleanup
If `--save-intermediates` wasn't passed, the temp directory (screenshots, audio chunks, progress JSON) is deleted via `fs.rmSync`.

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **Gemini File API** (`utils/gemini.ts`): All media is uploaded via `GoogleAIFileManager`, polled until processed, then referenced by URI in generation calls. Files are always cleaned up in a `finally` block.
- **No speech-to-text engine**: Transcription is done entirely by prompting the LLM with raw audio — the "experiment" referenced in the package description.
- **Overlapping chunks**: Audio chunks overlap by 1 minute (configurable) so the LLM doesn't lose speaker context at boundaries. The previous chunk's last 20 lines are also passed as text context.
- **Temperature tuning**: Transcription uses `0.2` (consistency), report sections use `0.3` (slight creativity), descriptions use `0` (deterministic).

message.content: What are the outputs of this?

content.text:
Based on the code I already read, here are all the outputs offmute produces:

### Primary Outputs (written to the input file's directory by default)

1. **`<filename>_transcription.md`** — Always produced. A markdown file containing:
   - File metadata (filename, creation/modification dates, processing date, file size, path, user instructions if any)
   - `# Meeting Description` — the merged description
   - `# Audio Analysis` — what Gemini inferred from the audio
   - `# Visual Analysis` — what Gemini inferred from screenshots (video only)
   - `# Full Transcription` — diarized transcript in `~[Speaker Name]~: text` format

2. **`<filename>_report.md`** — Only when `--report` is passed. A markdown file containing:
   - Report metadata (name, generation date, path)
   - `# Meeting Report` with dynamically generated sections (e.g. participant profiles, action items, contacts, discussion flow)

### Intermediate Outputs (only with `--save-intermediates`)

Saved to `.offmute_<filename>/` (or a custom dir via `--intermediates-dir`):

```
.offmute_<filename>/
├── screenshots/                  # Extracted JPG frames (video only)
│   └── <name>_screenshot_0.jpg ...
├── audio/
│   ├── <name>_tag_sample.mp3     # First 20min audio sample
│   └── <name>_chunk_0.mp3 ...    # 10min overlapping MP3 chunks
├── intermediate_output.json      # Image/audio/merged descriptions + prompts
├── config.json                   # Model, description, chunk count
└── transcription/
    ├── transcription_progress.json  # Per-chunk prompts + responses
    └── raw_transcriptions.json      # Array of raw chunk transcription strings
```

If `--report` is also used, an additional `.offmute/report/` directory is created in the output path:

```
.offmute/report/
└── report_generation.json     # Headings prompt, structure, per-section prompts + responses
```

### Console Output

- Progress bars (screenshots, audio processing, AI processing) during the description stage
- `Processing chunk N/M` logs during transcription
- `Processing section N/M: <title>` logs during report generation
- Final summary: total time, seconds-per-minute ratio, output file paths, success/failure counts

### Ephemeral (cleaned up unless `--save-intermediates`)

Without `--save-intermediates`, all intermediate files (screenshots, audio chunks, JSON progress files) are written to a temp directory (`os.tmpdir()`) and deleted via `fs.rmSync` after processing completes. Only the `.md` files persist.
